title: Puck of Pook's Hill
summaries: "Puck of Pook's Hill" by Rudyard Kipling is a fantasy book published in 1906 containing stories set across different periods of English history. When two children living in Sussex encounter the ancient elf Puck, he magically summons figures from England's past—Roman soldiers, Norman knights, Danish seafarers, and others—who share tales of conquest, adventure, and intrigue. Each story, bracketed by poems, reveals fragments of England's history, from Hadrian's Wall to the signing of Magna Carta, blending historical detail with magical elements.
